messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command configures the authentication mechanism used to control access to the network. The configured authentication method will be used to authenticate 802.1X clients. The no form of the command resets the authentication mechanism to the default, eap-radius. Specifies the EAP RADIUS as the 802.1X authentication meth od. Enabling the EAP RADIUS 802.1X authentication method on the switch: switch(config)# aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth- method eap-radius Resetting the EAP RADIUS 802.1X authentication method on the switch: switch(config)# no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth- method eap-radius NOTE For more information on features that use this command, use the search function within the AOS-CX doc portal. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command configures the switch to use an existing RADIUS server group for 802.1X authentication globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the server group to the default, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for 802.1X authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command enables periodic reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic reauthentication. Configures the period after which the authenticated clients are reauthenticated on the port. You must enable reauthentication on the port before configuring the reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 360 0. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Clears the 802.1X authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator clients attached to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all 802.1X enabled ports. Specifies the interface name. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access clients onboarding-method command do?

The show port-access clients onboarding-method command shows active port access client information for the specified onboarding method. Selects the onboarding method. Available methods: device-pr ofile, dot1x, mac-auth, port-security, proxy-mode. <METHOD> This command displays information related to traffic inspection. In the detailed view, inspection data for the clients currently onboarded in the system is displayed. In the summary view, inspection data per signature is summarized based on the system uptime. Show traffic inspection information. traffic-inspection interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the interface name. mac <MAC-ADDRESS> Specifies the client MAC address. method {<device-profile>| <dot1x>|<mac-auth>} Displays the information for a specific onboarded-method. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all MAC authentication enabled ports. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show running-config port-access vlan-group command do?

The show running-config port-access vlan-group command shows information for all configured VLAN groups. Configures the specified ports so they do not egress any packets that were received on the source port specified in interface context. The no form of this command removes the port filter setting from one or more ingress ports/LAGs. CAUTION This configuration will disable flow tracking statistics collection. Specifies a list of ports/LAGs to be blocked for egressing. Speci fy a single interface or LAG, or a range as a comma-separated list, or both. For example: 1/1/1, 1/1/3-1/1/6, lag2, lag1-lag4. On the HPE Aruba Networking 6400 Switch Se ries, interface identification differs. <INTERFACE-LIST> Displays filter settings for all interfaces or a specific interface. Specifies the ingress interface name. Syntax: show running-config port-access vlan-group.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. Public qos queue-profile 3609 Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show queue-monitor status command do?

The show queue-monitor status command displays the global state of the queue monitoring feature. The output includes the global enable/disable status, the memory consumed by the feature, the statistics being collected, and the interfaces currently enabled for monitoring. queue-monitor This command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to coll ect queue statistics at 10-second time intervals. These aggregate statistics ca n be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and performance anomalies. This feature is di sabled by default, but can be enabled on a maximum of 52 interfaces per switch. Syntax: show queue-monitor status.

The radius-proxy client-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the RADIUS proxy client group. A maximum of 32 RADIUS proxy client groups can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the configured client group. Specifies the name of the RADIUS proxy client group. Maximum of 32 characters. <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: no radius-proxy client-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show running-config (radius-proxy) command do?

The show running-config (radius-proxy) command displays the current running configuration. Configures the RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. The proxy waits for this time interval to get a response from the home server. This value should be equal to or greater than the NAS RADIUS timeout value. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS server timeout interval to its default value of 5 seconds. Public timeout (RADIUS proxy) 3754 RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. Default: 5 seconds. <SECONDS>. Syntax: show running-config.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no aaa accounting port-access command do?

The no aaa accounting port-access command configures port access accounting information that is captured for 802.1X and MAC-authenticated clients. Defines port access accounting as being local (with the parameter local) (the default). Or defines port access accounting as being remote (with the parameter group <GROUP-NAME>) with a sequence of remote RADIUS servers in a single RADIUS server group to be accessed for port access accounting purposes. For remote RADIUS port access accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote RADIUS server in the specified group. If a user-defined RADIUS server group is named in your command, it must exist. The no form of this command works as follows: • no aaa accounting port-access: Globally unconfigures port access accounting. Syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim.

syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa group server command do?

The aaa group server command creates an AAA server group that is either empty or contains preconfigured RADIUS/TACACS+ servers. You can create a maximum of 28 server groups. The no form of this command deletes a server group. Only a preconfigured user-defined RADIUS/TACACS+ server group can be deleted. RADIUS or TACACS+ servers that were in a deleted server group remain a part of their default server group. The default server group for TACACS+ servers is tacacs. The default server group for RADIUS servers is radius. server {tacacs | radius} Select either tacacs or radius for the server type. Specifies the name of the server group to be created. The name of the server group can have a maximum of 32 characters. Syntax: aaa group server {tacacs | radius} <SERVER-GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-server service-type in-access-request command do?

The radius-server service-type in-access-request command this configuration will include the Service-Type RADIUS attribute in Access-Request packets sent to a RADIUS server. The value of the attribute will always be NAS-Prompt-User. The no form of this command disables this feature. Syntax:
- no radius-server service-type in-access-request
- radius-server service-type in-access-request 3822.

The radius-server status-server interval <10-86400> command configures the time interval in seconds to send the status server requests to the RADIUS server. The no form of this command configures the default time interval, 300 seconds. Specifies the status server time interval in seconds. Default: 30 0. <10-86400>. Syntax:
- no radius-server status-server interval <10-86400>
- radius-server status-server interval 3823.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command do?

The radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before trying the next RADIUS server. If a value is not specified, a default value of 5 seconds is used. You can override this value with a fine-grained per server timeout configured for individual servers. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global authentication timeout to the default of 5 seconds. Specifies the timeout interval of 1 to 60 seconds. Default: 5 sec onds. Syntax: no radius-server timeout [<1-60>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies VRF name. Public RIP v2 Configuration commands 3911. Syntax: no router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The ip rip command enables RIP process on an interface. The no form of this command deletes the RIP process from an interface. ip rip <PROCESS-ID> Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. all-ip ip-address. Syntax: ip rip <PROCESS-ID> {all-ip | ip-address}.

The ip rip all-ip enable command enables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on the interface. Syntax:

- ip rip all-ip enable 3914.

The ip rip all-ip send disable command disables interface from sending RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. The no form of this command enables interface to send RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear ip rip statistics command do?

The clear ip rip statistics command clears RIP event statistics. Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63 <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs Clears statistics for all VRFs. Public RIP v2 clear commands 3928 Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. Enables RIP process for RIP enabled IP address configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on interface. Disables RIP process for RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process on interface. Disables an interface from sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. The no form of this command enables interface for sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. Public send disable (rip) 3932 Disables interface from receiving RIP packets for a specific IP address. Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIPng process ID. Range: <1-63> <PROCESS-ID> VRFsets VRF name for RIPng process. VRF name for VRF. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: no router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]; Public; RIPng (IPv6) commands 3951.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status rpvst command do?

The show capacities-status rpvst command shows the number of RPVST VLANs and RPVST VPORTs currently configured. Shows the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instances. When Port security is enabled on the port and the client is not-yet authenticated, the security feature keeps the port in the Down state. STP also keeps the port in the Blocking state and the role as Disabled in the show spanning-tree command output, whereas in the hardware, the state is maintained as Learning. After client authentication is successful, the port state changes to Forwarding. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities smartlink command do?

The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Enables the notification of events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. It is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the event traps. Enables sending SNMP traps for LLDP related events from a particular interface. LLDP trap generation is enabled by default on all the interfaces and has to be disabled for interfaces on which traps are not required to be generated. The no form of this command disables the LLDP trap generation. NOTE LLDP trap generation is disabled by default at the global level and must be enabled before any LLDP traps are sent. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show rmon alarm command do?

The show rmon alarm command displays the RMON alarm configurations. Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. Displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Displays all configured SNMP contexts. Displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command associates an SNMP MIB view with the SNMP community. The no form of this command removes the associated SNMP MIB view from the SNMP community. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. <STRING> <VIEW-NAME>. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server snmpv3-only command do?

The snmp-server snmpv3-only command accepts SNMP v3 messages only, SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c will be disabled. By default SNMP v1, SNMP v2c and SNMP v3 will all be enabled. The no form of this command restores the default setting and reenables SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c. Syntax:
- no snmp-server snmpv3-only
- snmp-server snmpv3-only 4234.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command do?

The snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command enables the SNMP trap for AAA server status. When enabled, traps are sent whenever AAA server (RADIUS, TACACS) status changes from reachable to unreachable and vice versa. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP trap for AAA server status. Syntax:
- no snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status
- snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status 4235.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show user-group command do?

The show user-group command shows user group information for the built-in groups plus any user-defined local user groups. When entered without <GROUP-NAME>, summary information is shown for all groups. Narrows the show command output to that of the specified gro up, and for local user groups, adds the User Group Rules list. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. <GROUP-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the following information for the logged-in user: • User name. • User authentication type: local, RADIUS, or TACACS+. • User group: administrators, operators, or <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: show user-group [<GROUP-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> command do?

The show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> command shows the global UBT state. Specifying a zone shows the UBT state of that zone. Specifying a UAC IP address shows the UBT state of that UAC. zone <ZONE-NAME> Specifies UBT zone name. Maximum characters: 64. uac-ip <UAC-ADDR> Specifies the IP address of the user anchor controller for which to view user information. Specify the address in IPv4 format (x. x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>.

The show vlan port command displays the VLANs configured for a specific layer 2 interface. <INTERFACE-ID> vsx-peer Specifies an interface ID. Format: member/slot/port. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4094. <VLAN-LIST>. Syntax: no vlan <VLAN-LIST>.

source_file: 6300_6400/10_17_1000/Command-line interface Reference Guide For 6300 and 6400 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan access command do?

The vlan access command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (Layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are non-routed (Layer 2) by default when created. Use routing and no routing commands to move ports between Layer 3 and Layer 2 interfaces. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4094. <VLAN-ID>. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME> command do?

The vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME> command adds protocol mapping to a VLAN on an interface. The no form of this command removes protocol mapping from the VLAN on an interface. Public vlan protocol 4733 Specifies a VLAN ID. Range: 2 to 4094. <VLAN-ID> <PROTOCOL_NAME>. Syntax: no vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show active-gateway <IFNAME> command do?

The show active-gateway <IFNAME> command displays the gateway information per SVI, such as: • Active-Gateway IPv4 and its MAC address • Active-Gateway IPv6 and its MAC address Specifies the VSX interface name. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Displays a virtual IPv4/IPv6 and MAC configured for active-active routing. Specifies the VLAN name. Syntax: string <VLAN-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show interface <VLAN-NAME> 4837 Displays a specified LAG or all configured LAGs along with VSX LAGs. Specifies the LAG name. Optional. Syntax: string <LAG-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. Syntax: show active-gateway <IFNAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx shutdown-on-split command do?

The vsx shutdown-on-split command shuts down the configured non-VSX interfaces on the VSX secondary along with VSX interfaces during a VSX split. The no form of this command resumes the non-VSX interfaces that are shut down during the VSX split. NOTE This command has no effect on the VSX primary during a split. However, when applied on the VSX primary, the command will bring down the non-VSX interfaces until linkup delay timer expires during the VSX primary reboot. Public vsx shutdown-on-split 4887. Syntax: no vsx shutdown-on-split.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. {[active-gateways] [policies]} access-gateways policies. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync arp-security command do?

The vsx-sync arp-security command enables VSX synchronization of the ARP security configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. After you enter vsx-sync arp-security, you must enter vsx-sync mclag-int erfaces for enabling VSX synchronization for the ARP security feature. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of ARP security configurations on VLAN mode and LAG interface mode to the secondary peer switch. However, it does not remove the existing ARP security configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync arp-security.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multip lier, min-transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. NOTE This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax:
- no vsx-sync bfd-global
- vsx-sync bfd-global 4904.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync dhcp-server command do?

The vsx-sync dhcp-server command enables VSX synchronization of all DHCPv4 server configurations, including external storage configurations, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. Only the primary VSX node answers DHCP service requests, and leases can only be exported from the primary VSX node. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 server configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv4 server feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-server.

The vsx-sync dhcp-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of DHCP snooping configurations on the primary node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize DHCP snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing DHCP snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax:

- vsx-sync dhcp-snooping 4911.

The vsx-sync evpn command enables syncing of all EVPN context-related configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. Public vsx-sync evpn 4915 The no form of this command disables syncing EVPN configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE As a prerequisite, VLAN vsx-sync must be enabled separately for the VLAN configurations inside EVPN context to get synced. Syntax: no vsx-sync evpn.

The no form of the command disables the VSX synchronization of IP ICMP configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the existing IP ICMP configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync icmp-tcp.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync keychain command do?

The vsx-sync keychain command enables synchronizing of key chain configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration synchronization from secondary to primary peer. If any additional modification or configuration is made on the primary for the key chain set of features, the features will be auto-synchronized. The no form of the command disables synchronizing key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synchronized configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync keychain.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command do?

The vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command enables the VSX synchronization of VSX LAG interface associations and attributes on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The Usage section in this topic provides a listing of specific associations and attributes that are synchronized to the secondary switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global VSX LAG and attributes, but it does not remove the existing VSX LAG feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces.

The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

The vsx-sync neighbor command enables VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. Public vsx-sync ospf 4927 The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync private-vlan-global command do?

The vsx-sync private-vlan-global command enable sync for private VLAN global configuration on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. Private VLAN global configuration syncing is supported on the switch by configuring the feature name under vsx configuration context. There are no parameters in this command. The no form of this command disables Private VLAN global configuration syncing to the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync private-vlan-global.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync qos-global command do?

The vsx-sync qos-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, such as CoS map, DSCP map, and trust policy, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync qos command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, but it does not remove the existing global QoS feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync qos-global.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. Public vsx-sync route-map 4934 The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE When vsx-sync for BGP or OSPF is configured, route map configurations are synchronized from primary VSX node to the secondary peer due to the dependency in configurations. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync stp-global command do?

The vsx-sync stp-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global STP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. Use the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command to sync context level spanning trees. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync mclag-inte rfaces command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global STP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global STP feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync stp-global.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync telnet command do?

The vsx-sync telnet command enables VSX synchronization of Telnet server configurations from the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize Telnet configurations associated with a particular VRF, you need to configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of Telnet server configurations to the secondary peer, however, it does not remove the existing Telnet server configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync telnet.

The vsx-sync time command enables VSX synchronization of time-related configurations, including NTP and time zone configurations, on the primary VSX node on the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global time-related configurations, but it does not remove the existing global time-related feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync time.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. Public vsx-sync vrrp 4946 The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erase all zeroize command do?

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. Syntax: erase [all] zeroize.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree mst-config command do?

The show spanning-tree mst-config command shows MSTP instance and corresponding VLAN information. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst-config [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command do?

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip forward-protocol udp command do?

The ip forward-protocol udp command defines the UDP server to which the interface forwards ingress UDP broadcast packets received on a specific UDP port. A maximum of 8 UDP broadcast servers can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes traffic forwarding for the specified server and port/protocol. <IPv4-ADDR> <PORT-NUM> <PROTOCOL> Specifies the UDP server IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), wh ere x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the UDP port number for which traffic is forwarded. Specifies the protocol name for which traffic is forwarded. Syntax: ip forward-protocol udp <IPv4-ADDR> {<PORT-NUM> | <PROTOCOL>}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities svi-count command do?

The show capacities svi-count command shows the maximum number of SVIs supported by the switch. The value displayed in the output will vary depending upon switch model and version of AOS-CX. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vlan voice command do?

The show vlan voice command displays the voice VLAN list showing the VLAN ID, name, operational state of the VLAN, and the interfaces associated with the VLAN. Disables a VLAN. (By default, a VLAN is automatically enabled when it is created with the vlan command.) The no form of this command enables a VLAN. Public shutdown (vlan) 4725. Syntax: show vlan voice.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan (interface) command do?

The vlan (interface) command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4094. <VLAN-LIST>. Syntax: vlan <VLAN-LIST>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router vrrp {enable disable} command do?

The router vrrp {enable disable} command enables or disables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. no router vrrp enable disables VRRP protocol globally but does not remove all VRRP configurations. no router vrrp disable enables VRRP protocol globally. Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no router vrrp command do?

The no router vrrp command removes VRRP configuration and VRRP global protocol. If auto-confirm is enabled or VRRP is not configured on any interface, this command will not ask for user confirmation. Syntax: no router vrrp.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface lag multi-chassis command do?

The interface lag multi-chassis command configures a given LAG as a dynamic multichassis LAG (VSX LAG), which supports a maximum of four member links per switch segment. A VSX LAG across a downstream switch can have at most a total of eight member links. The no form of this command removes a VSX LAG. Specifies the LAG ID. Run the show capacities vsx c ommand for the maximum number of VSX LAGs supported for your particular type of switch; however, the maximum VSX LAG value considers that one port is used for the ISL, which is not a VSX LAG. Required. Specifies the multichassis LAG as static. Optional. <LAG-ID> static. Syntax: interface lag <LAG-ID> multi-chassis [static].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip icmp redirect (VSX) command do?

The ip icmp redirect (VSX) command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Enabled by default. The no form of this command disables ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Syntax: ip icmp redirect.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command excludes the neighbor configurations from VSX sync operations at run time. When VSX sync is configured for BGP, the VSX secondary peer cannot have any configuration of its own. If the VSX primary has a configuration, that will always get synchronized to the VSX secondary. Any modification done in the synchronized configurations on the VSX secondary will be overwritten by the VSX primary configuration after a VSX config sync. If there is a new configuration on the VSX secondary, then that configuration also gets deleted after a VSX config sync. On the VSX primary, a configuration with vsx-sync-exclude enabled excludes the neighbor configurations from synchronizing to the VSX -secondary, allowing the VSX secondary can have it own entry for the corresponding neighbor configurations. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show active-gateway command do?

The show active-gateway command displays the gateway information configured on SVIs, such as: • Number of active-gateway interface VLANs • Number of IPv4 active-gateway interface VLANs • Number of IPv6 active-gateway interface VLANs • Per virtual MAC address ◦ ◦ IPv4 reference count and its interface VLANs IPv6 reference count and its interface VLANs Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show active-gateway [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show lacp interfaces (vsx) command do?

The show lacp interfaces (vsx) command displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. Optional: Specifies an interface name. <IFNAME> Public show lacp interfaces (vsx) 4840 Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. This example displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces. One of the interfaces has the lacp -block forwarding state. If a VSX switch has loop protect enabled on an interface and a loop occurs, VSX blocks the interface to stop the loop. Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vsx config-consistency command do?

The show vsx config-consistency command displays the VSX global configuration consistency between two VSX switches. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. The following example shows a comparison between the two VSX switches. Syntax: show vsx config-consistency [vsx-peer].

Specifies the datapath for an IPv6 address and its specified sub net. Optional. Syntax: A. B. C. D/M vrf <VRF-NAME> Shows the IPv6 datapath for a specified VRF. vsx-peer. Specifies the route information for an IPv4 address and its specif ied subnet. Optional. Syntax: A. B. C. messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vsx status command do?

The show vsx status command displays global VSX status or a specified status determined by the selected parameter. [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] Selects one of the following: inter-switch-link, kee palive, or linkup-delay inter-switch-link Specifies the display of the ISL status in VSX. keepalive Specifies the display of the VSX keepalive protocol status. linkup-delay vsx-peer Specifies the display of the VSX link-up delay information, suc h as the: • Configured link-up delay timer. • Delay timer status. Initial sync status. • • LAGs on which the delay timer is running. • Status of the LAGs excluded from the link-up delay timer. • • Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split. Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. Syntax: show vsx status [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vsx status peering command do?

The show vsx status peering command displays synchronization peering status and hardware peering status for modules such as MAC, neighbor, spanning-tree, and route. This command can be used to view the status of VSX peering, following a VSX device reboot or an ISL flap. Following are the possible values for VSX peering status: • Complete-VSX peering process is successfully completed. Public show vsx status peering 4878 • In-progress-VSX peering is being processed. • Not-started-VSX peering process is yet to be started. Syntax: show vsx status peering.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync (config-vlan-if context) command do?

The vsx-sync (config-vlan-if context) command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. {[active-gateways] [policies]} access-gateways policies. Syntax: vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show issu command do?

The show issu command shows information about the current state of ISSU. If no ISSU is currently in progress, the command displays the progress details of the last ISSU. Public show issu 4970 The command with the brief parameter displays a short summary of the ISSU state and indicates if the system is ready to accept an ISSU command and whether or not an ISSU is in progress. If the brief parameter is not included, then more details about an in progress ISSU or the last ISSU are displayed. Shows a short summary of the ISSU state. Shows details of ISSU software update history. Shows information about the current state of an ISSU validation. Syntax: show issu [brief|history|validation].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vsf topology command do?

The show vsf topology command displays information about VSF stack member connections. When the VSF links are of different interface speeds, the command ensures that all VSF interfaces in a stack operate at a chosen common denominator interface speed when there is at least one active stack member of type S0E91A or S0X44A in the stack. The no form of this command removes the common denominator speed from all VSF links and ensure that all the VSF links operate at their original speed. NOTE VSF egress-shape is enabled by default on the switch. This feature is applicable only for 6300 switch series (except for S3L75A, S3L76A and S3L77A). Enables egress shaping on all VSF interfaces. Public vsf egress-shape 4993 Forces the switch with non-factory default configuration to join a stack. Syntax: show vsf topology.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. Public interface vxlan 5007 (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Changes the inter-VXLAN bridging mode on tunnels where VXLAN bridging across tunnels is disabled by default. Default mode is deny. The no form of this command sets the inter-VXLAN bridging mode to the default mode of deny. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].

The syntax of the interface vxlan command is: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6]

Shows the VTEPS information for a specified VNI. transport-VRFshows the transport VRFstatus for a specified VNI. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Public show interface vxlan vteps 5036 Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag event-trap-disable command do?

The diag event-trap-disable command prevents the specific event notifications from being sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. The no form of this command disables this feature and reenables the notification of events to be sent as traps. Specify up to five comma separated Event IDs To modify the traps to be disabled, such as changing the existing list of event IDs or removing an event ID from the blocked trap list, first completely remove previously configured disabled traps using the CLI command no diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-ID>. After that, reconfigure the new event IDs or set of event IDs for the changes to take effect. Syntax: diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-IDS>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about IPv6 Example?

IPv6 Example: switch(config)# logrotate target tftp://2001: db8: 0: 1: : 128 Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path. Packet level failures with TFTP are handled in the protocol itself. With each TFTP session failure, TFTP retries the file transfer three times. Retries have a timeout of five seconds.

document_title: Diagnostics And Supportability Guide (6300, 6400 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Redundant Management communication?

Redundant Management communication: timeout The standby management module has taken over from an un responsive active management module. Redundant Management election timeout A failure to elect a standby management module in the alloted t ime. Critical service fault (error) A daemon critical to switch operation has stopped functioning. An extra error string may be present to describe the error in d etail. Chassis critical temperature Chassis operating temperature exceeded. Chassis insufficient fans Insufficient fans to cool the chassis. Chassis unsupported PSUs/fans Unsupported or misconfigured PSUs or system fans. Management module critical temperature Management module operating temperature exceeded. Uncontrolled reboots • ops-switchd crashed • ovsdb-server crashed Public Reboot reasons 43 • switchd_agent crashed Event Logs Event logging logs events generated by daemons, processes, and plug-ins running within the switch software. The event logging framework captures the event logs in a system journal by updating the journal fields and meta data.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy checkpoint command do?

The copy checkpoint command copies the checkpoint using TFTP, SFTP, SCP, or USB. Specifies the checkpoint name. <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE- URL>} Select either the storage URL or the remote URL for the destin ation of the copied command output. Required. Specifies the USB to copy command output. <STORAGE-URL> <REMOTE-URL> Specifies the URL to copy the command output. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE-URL>}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325/9300/9300S switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip command do?

The ip command shows or configures the port with a static IP address (IPv4 only) or enables the DHCP client on the port. An address is set only if a DHCP server is available to provide one. Public ip 182 {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR- NETMASK-GATEWAY>} show dhcp Selects the options for the OOBM port. Shows the OOBM port. Configures the port with a DHCP address. disable Disables the OOBM port. addr <ADDR-NETMASK- GATEWAY> Configures the port with a static IP address (IPv4 only). Specify address, netmask, and gateway as A. B. C. D. Syntax: ip {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR-NETMASK-GATEWAY>}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ls command do?

The ls command this command lists directory contents. Specifies options for the command. <OPTIONS> -1 -a -A -C -x -d -L -H -R -p -F -l -i Shows one-column output. Shows entries which start with a period (.). Shows output similar to -a, but excludes a period (.) and a dou ble period (..). Shows output list by columns. Shows output list by lines. Shows listing of directory entries instead of contents Follows symlinks. Follows symlinks on the command line. Recurse. Appends a slash (/) to directory entries. Appends an indicator to entries. An indicator can be as an aster isk (*) or slash (/) or equal sign (=) or at sign (@) or pipe (|). Shows the output in a long listing format. Shows the list inode numbers. Syntax: ls [<OPTIONS>] [<FILE-NME>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the system serviceos password-prompt command do?

The system serviceos password-prompt command use this command to enable password authentication for ServiceOS. By default, the ServiceOS shell (accessible only from the local switch console port) requires no password to login as an admin use. When this setting is enabled, the same password used to authenticate the admin user in the AOS-CX CLI or WebUI can be used to log in to the ServiceOS shell. If this setting is enabled, a forgotten admin user password cannot be reset using ServiceOS; if there are no other local or RADIUS/TACACS user accounts with administrator-level access, the switch must be zeroized by entering the username zeroize command at the ServiceOS login prompt to restore administrator access. serviceos password-prompt cannot be enabled if the built-in admin user has been disabled. Syntax: system serviceos password-prompt.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command do?

The tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command transfers files to and from a remote machine (TFTP a file). Public tftp 202 {-b | -g | -l | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} Selects the options for transferring a file. -b -g -l -p Specifies the transfer blocks of size octets. The default blocksi ze is set to 1468, which can be overridden with the -b option. Specifies to get a file. Specifies a local file. Specifies to put a file in remote location. -r <REMOTE-FILE> Specifies a remote file. Specifies the port for transfer. If no port option is specified, TFT P uses the standard UDP port 69 by default. <PORT>. Syntax: tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>].

The version command displays the following build strings: • Version. • Build date. • Build time. • Build ID. • SHA. Syntax: version.
